title: LLM Evaluation Stack for Production Risk Mitigation
situation: Independent consulting engagement required robust evaluation of high-volume daily LLM generations to prevent hallucinations, latency spikes, and cost overruns in production.
task: Stand up an end-to-end LLM evaluation stack to monitor hallucination, latency, and cost across production LLM pipelines.
action: Built and deployed a stack using Langfuse and Phoenix to score key metrics; integrated into CI/CD workflows; simulated production conditions to validate error budgets.
result: Reduced error budget breaches by 38% in simulated production environment, enabling proactive risk mitigation.

title: Cloud-Native Modernisation of Core Banking Platforms
situation: ANZ’s core banking platforms were monolithic, resulting in slow delivery cycles and high infrastructure costs, impeding agility and innovation.
task: Orchestrate enterprise-wide transformation of core banking systems from monolith to cloud-native (.NET/Azure), aligning with enterprise architecture standards and reducing time-to-market.
action: Led a $5M+ program portfolio across 5+ cross-functional squads (up to 40 resources, including offshore teams); defined technical vision, managed product backlog, and enforced architectural governance; facilitated executive workshops to align 40+ GMs on strategy.
result: Cut end-to-end delivery time by >30%; reduced infrastructure costs by >15%; achieved 100% compliance with enterprise architectural standards and risk models; improved decision-making efficiency and project clarity by >55%.

title: Automating COBOL/Mainframe Test Evidence for Payday Super Reform
situation: The Australian Taxation Office's Payday Super reform program faced a mathematically infeasible SIT window: 75+ hours of manual evidence per team against only 64 available hours, threatening on-time delivery of mission-critical regulatory changes.
task: Architect and implement a test automation strategy to reduce manual evidence effort across 200+ SIT/E2E scenarios for all eight Agile Kookaburras squads, while enabling executive re-baselining of test capacity.
action: Designed and built a zero-new-approvals toolchain using REXX, SMF, SDSF, PCOMM, PowerShell, and VBA to automate evidence collection; led a six-day tiered harness build with formal go/no-go gates and a four-level contingency ladder; authored an executive change request to re-baseline test capacity from 30 to 90 person-days, including full Azure DevOps traceability across 40 scenarios and 11 data tables.
result: Reduced evidence effort from ~3 hours to ~15 minutes per scenario (≈92% reduction); converted an infeasible plan into an achievable one; unblocked stalled NTP function testing via L2 environment escalation and targeted SME enablement; delivered Distribution UI to 95%+ completion.

title: Production-Grade Public-Key Server for Fleet API Signing
situation: Fleet API operations required a secure, reliable mechanism for distributing PEM keys for signing requests across distributed services.
task: To build a production-grade Node.js/Express service for public-key distribution with full test coverage.
action: Designed and implemented a robust key distribution service using Node.js/Express, with automated testing via Mocha/Chai.
result: Achieved 100% test coverage and delivered a production-ready service for fleet API signing.

title: JIRA Analytics Dashboard for Agile Team Insights
situation: Agile teams lacked visibility into sprint velocity and retrospective trends, limiting data-driven decision-making.
task: To build a self-service analytics dashboard exposing sprint metrics and LLM-powered retrospective insights.
action: Architected and developed a Next.js + Supabase application to visualise sprint velocity and generate LLM-powered retrospective insights and sprint plans.
result: Enabled teams to track performance and improve planning cadence with automated insights.

title: AI/ML Strategy and LLM Evaluation Stack at ANZ & Independent
situation: ANZ and independent consulting engagements required robust MLOps and LLM evaluation capabilities to manage hallucination, latency, and cost across high-volume daily LLM generations.
task: To stand up an end-to-end LLM evaluation stack and deliver AI solutions aligned with enterprise MLOps and compliance standards.
action: Built and deployed a Langfuse + Phoenix evaluation stack to score hallucination, latency, and cost. Also delivered AI solutions including real-time telemetry servers and LLM-powered analytics tools.
result: Reduced error budget breaches by 38% in simulated production; supported AI/ML strategy across financial services.

title: Executive Change Request to Triple Test Capacity for Payday Super Program
situation: The ATO’s Payday Super program required a formal reassessment of test capacity due to scaling demands; baseline capacity was 30 person-days, but the program needed up to 90 person-days to meet aggressive timelines.
task: To author and secure executive approval for a change request that rebaselined test capacity from 30 to 90 person-days, including full risk assessment, options analysis, and traceability.
action: Authored a comprehensive executive change request covering costed recommendations, risk assessment, options analysis, and end-to-end Azure DevOps traceability across 40 scenarios and 11 data tables (AC6–AC19), including STT regression scope.
result: Change request approved, enabling capacity scaling to 90 person-days and ensuring test coverage met program compliance and timeline requirements.

title: Building Production-Grade Public-Key Server for Fleet API Signing
situation: Fleet API clients required secure, scalable distribution of PEM keys for signing requests.
task: Develop a robust, production-grade Node.js/Express service for public-key distribution.
action: Built and deployed a production-grade Node.js/Express service with full test coverage using Mocha/Chai.
result: Achieved 100% test coverage and ensured secure, reliable key distribution.

title: Unblocking NTP Function Testing via Cross-Discipline War Room
situation: NTP function testing was stalled due to environment and SME availability issues, threatening Distribution UI delivery timelines.

result: Distribution UI delivery achieved 95%+ completion, and NTP testing was unblocked.

title: Executive Change Request to Triple Test Capacity for Payday Super
situation: Executive governance required formal re-baselining of test capacity for the Payday Super program due to under-resourced testing (30 person-days) against actual demand.
task: Prepare and secure executive approval to increase test capacity from 30 to up to 90 person-days, including risk assessment and full traceability.

result: Successfully secured executive approval, enabling scaled test execution and supporting program delivery under revised capacity.

title: Architecting COBOL/Mainframe Test Automation Reducing Effort by 92%
situation: The Payday Super reform program required test evidence automation for 200+ SIT/E2E scenarios across eight squads, with manual evidence taking ~3 hours per scenario.
task: Design and implement a zero-new-approvals test automation toolchain to drastically reduce manual effort while ensuring traceability and compliance.
action: Architected and deployed an automation framework using REXX, SMF, SDSF, PCOMM, PowerShell, and VBA to automate evidence generation across all scenarios.
result: Reduced evidence effort from ~3 hours to ~15 minutes per scenario (≈92% reduction), enabling faster feedback and higher test throughput.

title: Recovering Infeasible SIT Window for Payday Super Reform Program
situation: The Australian Taxation Office's Payday Super reform program faced a mathematically infeasible System Integration Testing (SIT) window: 75+ hours of manual evidence per team against only 64 available hours.
task: Convert the infeasible SIT plan into an achievable delivery schedule while maintaining test coverage and compliance requirements.
action: Designed and implemented a six-day tiered test harness build with a formal go/no-go gate and a four-level contingency ladder to absorb schedule slippage and resource constraints.
result: Successfully transformed the infeasible plan into a realistic and executable SIT schedule, enabling on-time delivery of test execution within the 64-hour window.
